article_items.title: NVIDIA Accelerates Google DeepMind’s DiffusionGemma for Local AI
article_items.description: The new DiffusionGemma open model generates text in parallel - not one token at a time - and is optimized to run on the NVIDIA RTX PRO platform, NVIDIA DGX Spark systems and GeForce RTX GPUs.

Building Windows agents just got a full toolset - NVIDIA and Microsoft rolled out turnkey agent sandboxing on native Windows - Microsoft eXecution Containers plus the NVIDIA OpenShell runtime - alongside up to 2x faster.

At just 2.6 billion parameters, its distilled version generates a full 60-second clip in 34 seconds on a single NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 GPU using the NVFP4 format - delivering up to 36x higher throughput than comparable.
The fastest way to start testing and prototyping the model is through Hugging Face Transformers, which runs DiffusionGemma on a GeForce RTX 5090 or DGX Spark out of the box.
On NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 workstations, providing developers, researchers and AI professionals with the headroom to run local low-latency generation and agentic loops as part of a professional workflow.
On DGX Station, delivering best-in-class, high-speed inference at up to 800 tokens/sec for low-latency text generation and agentic loops with 748GB of coherent memory.

And the new cluster assistant in NVIDIA Sync links up to four DGX Spark units into one 512GB pool - enough for ~400-billion-parameter models.
In short, the model’s design plays directly to the GPU ’ ‘ s strengths.

article_items.title: AI amplifies creativity by removing friction | The Microsoft Cloud Blog
article_items.description: An executive perspective on how AI is reshaping creative work - and why the outcome depends not just on the technology, but on how it’s applied across teams.
article_items.excerpt: An executive perspective on how AI is reshaping creative work - and why the outcome depends not just on the technology, but on how it’s applied across teams.
article_items.summary: And the organizations navigating this well are balancing their need for oversight of AI tools with a clear focus on the conditions that allow strong creative work to emerge. In her role leading integrated marketing for Commercial Cloud and AI, she sees AI not as something that diminishes creativity, but as something that reflects the clarity - or ambiguity - behind the work. That’s where leadership matters - helping set direction, align teams, and establish the guardrails that allow creative work to scale without losing its distinctiveness. When leaders participate alongside their teams - testing, learning, and iterating - it helps normalize change and build momentum. Creativity expands when space is protected That shift matters because creativity requires time, focus, and energy - resources that are often consumed by repetitive work.

article_items.title: Meta signs first AI data center deal in India with Reliance | TechCrunch
article_items.description: The 168-megawatt facility will support Meta's global AI computing needs and can be expanded over time.
article_items.excerpt: The 168-megawatt facility will support Meta's global AI computing needs and can be expanded over time.
article_items.summary: Earlier this week, Blackstone-backed AirTrunk announced plans to invest $30 billion to build 5 gigawatts of data center capacity in the country by 2030, while Indian conglomerates, including Adani and Tata Consultancy Services , have also unveiled major data center expansion plans aimed at supporting AI workloads. The 168-megawatt facility will support Meta's global AI computing needs and can be expanded over time. Further, the data center will also support Meta’s global infrastructure and AI computing requirements, plugging India more directly into the company’s worldwide network of AI facilities. The deal comes as India cements its status as a natural destination for AI infrastructure investments, with tech giants seeking new geographies for data centers amid soaring demand for computing power to train and.

Separately, Meta said it had contracted nearly 1 gigawatt of new renewable energy capacity in India through agreements with CleanMax and Fourth Partner Energy, which will supplement the renewable power supporting the.
India’s installed data center capacity has risen from about 375 megawatts in 2020 to around 1.5 gigawatts in 2025, according to government data.

Several companies, including Microsoft , Amazon , Google , OpenAI , and Uber , have recently announced AI and cloud infrastructure investments in the country, which has rapidly expanded its data center footprint in.

The partnership, announced on Wednesday, will see Meta collaborate with Reliance on a 168-megawatt AI-enabled data center in Jamnagar, Gujarat, expanding a relationship that has evolved from Meta’s multibillion-dollar.

article_items.title: The Missing Link Between Agents and Applications
article_items.description: Most AI agent tools run on servers, limiting access to browser APIs, device capabilities, and frontend state. Discover how LangChain headless tools enable secure client-side tool execution for modern agent applications.
article_items.excerpt: Most AI agent tools run on servers, limiting access to browser APIs, device capabilities, and frontend state. Discover how LangChain headless tools enable secure client-side tool execution for modern agent applications.
article_items.summary: The browser is where many high-value agent actions actually happen: reading local application state, acting on the current UI, and using device capabilities without shipping that data to a backend first. Agents can interact with the user's environment directly, reducing round trips and allowing sensitive data to remain local by default. The agent can reason about the user's request on the server, but the document model, selection state, and editing commands all live in the client. The benefit is not just "browser access." Imagine an agent helping you work through a slide deck: it should be able to jump to the active slide, read local context, and update the presentation in place without shipping the whole session to a backend. The difference is what happens next. The Missing Link Between Agents and Applications Most agent tools only see the backend.
article_items.what_happened: The browser is where many high-value agent actions actually happen: reading local application state, acting on the current UI, and using device capabilities without shipping that data to a backend first. The Missing Link Between Agents and Applications Most agent tools only see the backend.
But behind the scenes, the server and the client are coordinating: the server knows what the agent wants to do, and the client knows how to do it.
When a model decides to use a tool, the agent runs it in-process or delegates it to an external service such as an MCP server, then feeds the result back into the reasoning loop.

The benefit is not just "browser access." Imagine an agent helping you work through a slide deck: it should be able to jump to the active slide, read local context, and update the presentation in place without shipping.
The client runs the tool locally and sends the result back, and the agent picks up where it left off.
S e e w h a t y o u r a g e n t i s r e a l l y d o i n g LangSmith, our agent engineering platform, helps developers debug every agent decision, eval changes, and deploy in one click.

Headless tools make those interactions available while preserving typed schemas, explicit capabilities, structured outputs, and reviewability, allowing agents to use tools that are native to the user, not just.
The model never needs to know where the tool runs.
Headless tools put client-side actions inside the agent's reasoning loop, not alongside it.

article_items.title: Introducing LangChain Labs
article_items.description: LangChain Labs is a new applied research effort focused on continual learning for agents, with partners advancing open research on self-improving AI systems.
article_items.excerpt: LangChain Labs is a new applied research effort focused on continual learning for agents, with partners advancing open research on self-improving AI systems.
article_items.summary: Some early work with our partners includes measuring how agents generalize between different vertical domains (like legal services); harness engineering & fine-tuning open models like Nemotron as cost-efficient subagents; and building evals/environments so teams can turn their trace data into usable data to improve agents. LangChain Labs is a new applied research effort focused on continual learning for agents, with partners advancing open research on self-improving AI systems. For many of the world’s most important tasks, we’re yet to discover the most efficient combination of models harnesses, models, and feedback loops that allow agents to self-improve. S e e w h a t y o u r a g e n t i s r e a l l y d o i n g LangSmith, our agent engineering platform, helps developers debug every agent decision, eval changes, and deploy in one click. Our goal is to advance more open research powering the next generation of self-improving agents. Improving Agents by Mining Information from Large-Scale Agent Data: Agents are being integrated into software systems at a rapid rate.

Our goal is to advance more open research powering the next generation of self-improving agents.
We want to partner with teams looking to explore how agents learn, adapt, and improve.
The goal is to help agents improve from the data they already produce, including traces, feedback, eval results, and production behavior.
Traces are the source of that data and we want to help every team use traces to build better agents.
We’re starting this work with a few early research partners including Harvey, Nvidia, Prime Intellect, Fireworks, and Baseten.

Google confirmed at 3 a.m.
A "mitigation" or fix is on the way, Google confirmed at 9:24 a.m.
So when I see something like "Gemini is down," my mind goes to all of the Google products that have integrated the AI system over the past few years.
Google still hasn't given us many details about the underlying issue, but it did confirm that the error codes 1099 and 1076 are part of the issue.

I had the flash model selected, which I immediately knew wouldn't be the best model for the task after I hit "run." But I didn't have to wait long to fix my error, because Gemini couldn't run the task at all.
PT that some fixes to the Gemini outage are on the way.

I checked my Google Health app, which is what the old Fitbit app has become, and had a conversation with the chatbot there about why the fact that I feel like I'm coming down with a cold isn't showing up in my biometric.
PT that a fix is on the way, but there's no ETA on when it may be deployed.
Google said it does not yet know the cause of the issue.
Google said it will issue another update at 9:30 a.m.
Most likely, the Google engineering teams caught the issue late last night or early this morning, before many of us logged on for work at 8 a.m.

article_items.title: New framework for auditing machine unlearning
article_items.excerpt: Explore our many areas of focus Building a collaborative ecosystem Shaping the future together Translating discovery into real-world impact Explore our many areas of focus Building a collaborative ecosystem Shaping the future together Translating discovery into real-world impact New framework for auditing machine unlearning We introduce a method designed to confidently determine whether there is statistically significant evidence that two sets of data observations come from entirely different underlying distributions. As models process increasingly massive and highly sensitive datasets, verifying machine unlearning has moved from theoretical ideal to a strict requirement, where...
article_items.summary: Our test acts as a highly adaptable statistical toolkit that leverages f-divergences to allow auditors to pinpoint highly specific types of data shifts, including: Chi-squared and Kullback-Liebler (KL) divergences: These are highly effective for identifying smooth and localized differences in data, such as outliers in physical models. Because it directly aligns with the mathematical foundations of pure differential privacy, it allows auditors to tightly control the acceptable degree of data shift. We then shifted our primary focus to the critical, real-world applications of auditing differential privacy and evaluating machine unlearning: Privacy auditing : Differential privacy provides a framework for protecting user data by introducing calibrated noise, bounding the influence of any single individual. While popular standard tools like maximum mean discrepancy (MMD) excel at detecting broad, global shifts across data. Our test evaluated whether the unlearned model distribution was closer to the safe gold standard model, or closer to the original, fully trained model that actively memorized the sensitive data.

rag_rules: Separate proven capability from claimed capability.
Name the benchmark, model, dataset, or method when available.
Explain the user-facing meaning of the technical result.
State the rule, complaint, restriction, or risk plainly.
